underlying: University of Michigan Index of Consumer Sentiment (overall, final monthly release, FRED UMCSENT)
method: RULES VERIFIED via Kalshi API: 'Market resolves YES if any University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index final monthly release published during 2026 reports a value exceeding 65.0' — early-close lookback over 8 remaining final releases (May 30, Jun 27, Jul 25, Aug 29, Sep 26, Oct 31, Nov 28, Dec 19, 2026). Prior theo treated this as a single-shot Dec terminal print, materially understating P(YES). Recompute as P(max of 8 monthly final prints > 65). Latest finals: Apr26 49.8 (record low), Mar26 53.3, Feb26 56.6, Jan26 56.4, Dec25 52.9, Nov25 51.0, Oct25 53.6, Sep25 55.1, Aug25 58.2, Jul25 61.7. Conference Board Consumer Confidence April 2026 = 92.8 (edged UP, ceasefire-driven recovery), diverging from UMich record low — alt-data signal that some bounce is plausible. Modeling: drift mu_t from 50 in May to ~58 by Dec (mean reversion on Iran-shock fading); per-release sigma ~= 4-6 (rises with horizon). Single-release P(X > 65) ranges from ~0.5% in May (mu=50, sig=4) to ~13% in Dec (mu=58, sig=6). With strong serial correlation (rho ~0.7), effective independent draws ~2.5-3, so P(max > 65) ~= 17-19%. Use 0.18 as central estimate.
notes: REVISION: corrected rules interpretation (lookback, not single-shot Dec). Confirmed via Kalshi API: market is 'above 65.0 in any 2026 final release', early-close on trigger. P(YES) raised from 0.14 to 0.18 to reflect the 8-release max. Market YES @ 17c is approximately fair vs my 18%, modest YES edge. ALT-DATA refinement: (1) Conference Board CCI Apr26 = 92.8 (rose 0.6pt; expectations index +1.2 to 72.2), suggests UMich record low partly Iran-survey-window artifact and some retracement likely; (2) Langer Consumer Comfort Index weekly is at 1.5yr low ~38.9 — confirms broad weakness, no strong leading bounce; (3) Morning Consult ESI daily shows similar pattern. Net read: real consumer sentiment IS depressed, but UMich is at the low end of the survey panel and most likely to retrace toward the 55-60 mid-50s regime when Iran-conflict survey questions stabilize. To clear 65 still requires a ~15pt swing from current 50, which has happened 5 times in the post-2008 record (typically post-crisis recoveries). Confidence MEDIUM (raised from low for rules, kept moderate for path uncertainty). Key risks: prolonged Iran/oil shock keeps mu_t pinned in low 50s (lowers P), or a sharp ceasefire+disinflation rally pushes a single print above 65 (raises P). Sources: Kalshi API (rules), investinglive/Bloomberg/CNN (April final 49.8), Conference Board press release (April CCI 92.8), Langer Research (weekly CCI).
